response.justification: Bogdan has genuine geospatial and GIS domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map, and his time at Dunnhumby shows he can work on high-throughput distributed systems. However, this role is fundamentally a C++ or Rust systems-engineering position focused on routing tile pipelines and navigation data processing, and Bogdan's C++ experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments over a decade ago plus a bachelor thesis project. His current and recent work is overwhelmingly TypeScript/Node.js/web-focused, with no evidence of systems-level programming, routing tile generation, or navigation guidance development.
strengths: Geospatial domain familiarity through GISCollective and Green Map — understands map data, GIS concepts, and open geospatial tooling
Distributed microservices experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second shows comfort with high-throughput, low-latency systems
Polyglot background and track record of picking up unfamiliar technologies (Swift, DLang, Java, C++) suggests adaptability
concerns: No meaningful modern C++ or Rust experience — C++ appears only in a 2014 thesis project and a brief 2014–2015 role; primary languages are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang
GIS experience is web-application layer (Ember.js, Node.js REST APIs), not routing tile pipelines, map data transformation, or navigation algorithms
No exposure to routing systems, navigation guidance solutions, or geospatial data pipelines at the infrastructure level the role requires
